sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a complaint of back pain and tingling sensation in the right leg, which started after raking leaves in the yard for 2 hours.
The pain is worse when standing up and improves with sitting and a hot shower.
The patient has had similar back pain in the past, but it resolved within a day.
The patient's partner reports similar pain when the patient is standing up.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has some pain on flexion of the low back and pain to palpation around the L5 region.
Dorsiflexion is normal, and pulses are equal in all extremities.
The patient has a tingling sensation in the right leg.
Test Results: The patient had a normal X-ray of the low back.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely sprained their low back.
Problem list: Low back sprain; suspected nerve compression; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg orally once daily for pain.
I also prescribed physical therapy to get the patient back in shape, and to provide stretches and exercises.
If the patient's symptoms do not improve, I will consider ordering an MRI in two weeks.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in two weeks to re-evaluate their symptoms.
